content:
{
  "title": "Adversarial Prompt Immunization via Dynamic Contextual Reinforcement",
  "problem": "Current language models are vulnerable to adversarial prompts and jailbreak attempts that elicit harmful or unauthorized outputs. Static or rigid prompt defense mechanisms often fail to generalize to novel attack strategies.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs become increasingly deployed in sensitive, real-world applications, adversarial robustness is critical. Most existing approaches focus on filtering outputs or regularizing model training, but prompt-level defenses remain underexplored. Prompting techniques that adaptively disrupt adversarial strategies could dramatically improve model safety without retraining.",
  "method": "We propose a prompting method that dynamically augments the user prompt with contextually sampled, semantically similar distractors drawn from a large, safe corpus. These distractors are selected based on their ability to steer model generation away from adversarial intent, leveraging a retrieval-augmented adversarial alignment module. The augmented prompt is constructed in real-time by interleaving user query segments with distractors in a manner that maintains task relevance for benign inputs but disrupts adversarial composition. This approach transforms the prompt structure on-the-fly, reducing the success of jailbreak patterns reliant on rigid input-output mapping.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on jailbreak and adversarial prompt benchmarks such as AdvBench, RealToxicityPrompts, and custom attack sets. Baselines include standard prompting, prompt-chaining, and output filtering approaches. Metrics are attack success rate, task completion for benign prompts, and model utility retention. Ablations will test distractor selection, placement, and semantic similarity thresholds."
}
